# Copilot Instructions for AcadHomepage

## Project Overview
This is **AcadHomepage**, a Jekyll-based academic personal website built with automation for Google Scholar citation tracking. It's a GitHub Pages site forking the [acad-homepage](https://github.com/RayeRen/acad-homepage.github.io) template, customized for an academic researcher's CV and portfolio.

**Key Purpose**: Serve as Dongming Jin's academic homepage with automatic Google Scholar publication/citation updates via GitHub Actions.

## Architecture & Components

### 1. **Jekyll Static Site Generator**
- **Build Tool**: Ruby + Jekyll with GitHub Pages
- **Config**: [_config.yml](_config.yml) — Site title, author info, analytics setup
- **Server**: Run `bash run_server.sh` (executes `bundle exec jekyll liveserve`) for local development at `http://127.0.0.1:4000`
- **Gems**: [Gemfile](Gemfile) — Uses `github-pages` gem for GH Pages compatibility; includes jekyll-feed, jekyll-sitemap, hawkins (livereload)

### 2. **Content Structure**
- **Main Page**: [_pages/about.md](_pages/about.md) — Homepage with About, News, and Publications sections (permalink: `/`)
- **Layouts**: [_layouts/default.html](_layouts/default.html) — Master template with sidebar, masthead, and main content
- **Components**: [_includes/](_includes/) — Reusable HTML blocks (head, masthead, sidebar, author-profile, scripts)
- **Styles**: [_sass/](_sass/) — SCSS files for responsive design; uses vendor libraries (Breakpoint, Font Awesome, Susy grid)

### 3. **Google Scholar Integration** ⭐ **Critical Automation**
- **Crawler**: [google_scholar_crawler/main.py](google_scholar_crawler/main.py)
  - Uses `scholarly` library to fetch citation data from Google Scholar
  - Reads `GOOGLE_SCHOLAR_ID` environment variable (set via GitHub Actions secret)
  - Outputs: `gs_data.json` (full author data) and `gs_data_shieldsio.json` (citation count for badges)
  
- **Automation**: [.github/workflows/google_scholar_crawler.yaml](.github/workflows/google_scholar_crawler.yaml)
  - **Triggers**: On page build + daily at 08:00 UTC (`0 8 * * *`)
  - **Flow**: Checkout repo → Install Python deps → Run crawler → Push results to `google-scholar-stats` branch
  - **Result**: Citation data cached on a separate git branch, CDN-served via jsdelivr

- **Frontend Integration**: [_pages/about.md](_pages/about.md) lines 11-16
  - Conditionally uses CDN or raw GitHub URLs based on `google_scholar_stats_use_cdn` config
  - `<span class='show_paper_citations' data='PAPER_ID'></span>` displays paper-level citations

## Developer Workflows

### Local Development
```bash
bundle exec jekyll liveserve  # Watch & auto-reload on file changes
# Open http://127.0.0.1:4000 in browser
```

### Publishing Content
1. Edit Markdown in `_pages/about.md` (News section, Publications, About sections)
2. Use Jekyll front matter for layout/permalink control
3. For citations: Get Google Scholar paper ID from citation view URL, insert in `<span>` tag with `data` attribute
4. Commit and push to `main` branch — site auto-publishes via GitHub Pages

### Citation Data Updates
- Automatic: Runs daily or on push
- Manual: Disable GH Actions secrets if testing locally (scholar library requires network)
- Note: `gs_data.json` lives on `google-scholar-stats` branch, not main

## Project-Specific Conventions

### SEO & Analytics
- Optional setup: `google_site_verification`, `bing_site_verification`, `baidu_site_verification` in [_config.yml](_config.yml) (lines 18-20)
- Google Analytics ID: Add via config (line 13)
- Site Maps & Feed: Auto-generated by jekyll-sitemap and jekyll-feed plugins

### Author Profile Customization
- [_config.yml](_config.yml) lines 23-61: All author metadata (name, avatar, bio, social links)
- Avatar: `images/android-chrome-512x512.png` (generated via favicon-generator tool)
- Supported Links: GitHub, LinkedIn, Google Scholar, DBLP, ORCID, ResearchGate, etc.

### File Organization
- **Build Exclusions**: [_config.yml](_config.yml) line 62-90 — google_scholar_crawler, docs, plugins, vendor, node_modules excluded from site build
- **Assets**: `assets/css/` (main.scss + compiled CSS), `assets/js/` (main JS + plugins like magnific-popup for lightbox)

## Critical Files & Patterns

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| [_pages/about.md](_pages/about.md) | Single-page site content; edit here for News/Publications |
| [_config.yml](_config.yml) | Site-wide config; restart server after editing |
| [google_scholar_crawler/main.py](google_scholar_crawler/main.py) | Fetches/stores scholar data; requires GOOGLE_SCHOLAR_ID env var |
| [_layouts/default.html](_layouts/default.html) | Master HTML template with sidebar layout |
| [_sass/_page.scss](_sass/_page.scss) | Styles for main content area |

## Common Tasks

**Add a new publication**:
- Edit [_pages/about.md](_pages/about.md) Publication section
- For citation badges: Get Paper ID from Scholar, add `<span class='show_paper_citations' data='PAPER_ID'></span>`

**Update news/bio**:
- Edit News or About sections in [_pages/about.md](_pages/about.md)
- Reload at http://127.0.0.1:4000 (automatic with liveserve)

**Change site title/description**:
- Modify `title`, `description`, `author` in [_config.yml](_config.yml)
- Restart Jekyll server (liveserve doesn't auto-reload config)

**Debug citation updates**:
- Check [.github/workflows/google_scholar_crawler.yaml](.github/workflows/google_Scholar_crawler.yaml) logs in Actions tab
- Verify `GOOGLE_SCHOLAR_ID` secret is set in repo Settings
- Verify gs_data.json exists on `google-scholar-stats` branch

## Deployment
- **Hosting**: GitHub Pages (username.github.io)
- **Auto-Deploy**: Any push to `main` triggers Jekyll build; published immediately
- **Citation Data**: Separate `google-scholar-stats` branch auto-updated daily/on push
